name: bitget-wallet-skill
description: AI agent skill for Bitget Wallet — token swap, cross-chain bridge, and gasless transactions via Order Mode API. Supports 7 EVM chains + Solana.

commit.message:
docs: sync skill with official API documentation (#28)

* docs: sync with official API docs — chains, error codes, security labels, gasless thresholds

* refactor: replace noGas/no_gas concept references with gasless

Unify terminology: use 'gasless' as the concept name throughout docs.
API parameter values (--feature no_gas, features: ["no_gas"]) remain unchanged.

Files: CHANGELOG.md, SKILL.md, docs/trading.md, docs/wallet-signing.md, scripts/bitget_api.py

* docs: replace noGas with Gasless in CHANGELOG

* fix: revert --feature help text to original

* docs: replace noGas Min column headers with Gasless Min

commit.message:
fix: Solana gasless IS supported (min ~$5-6 threshold) (#25)

Corrected previous conclusion that Solana didn't support gasless.
Testing confirmed:
- Same-chain: 6 USDC → USDT gasless ✅
- Cross-chain: 20 USDC Sol→Base gasless ✅
- Minimum amount threshold: ~$5-6 USD

Below threshold: features=[], above: features=["no_gas"].
Updated trading.md, wallet-signing.md, README.md, CHANGELOG.

verification.payload:

parent 1e7390b7e508a8ea119b60f7269f6f627c6209bc
author bgw-dev-pub-agent <bgw-dev-pub-agent@bitget.com> 1773050170 -0700
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1773050170 +0800

fix: Solana gasless IS supported (min ~$5-6 threshold) (#25)

Corrected previous conclusion that Solana didn't support gasless.
Testing confirmed:
- Same-chain: 6 USDC → USDT gasless ✅
- Cross-chain: 20 USDC Sol→Base gasless ✅
- Minimum amount threshold: ~$5-6 USD

Below threshold: features=[], above: features=["no_gas"].
Updated trading.md, wallet-signing.md, README.md, CHANGELOG.

commit.message:
feat: add Solana partial-sign support to order_sign.py (#22)

* feat: add Solana partial-sign support to order_sign.py

- Add sign_solana_tx() for VersionedTransaction partial sign with Legacy fallback
- Add _load_sol_keypair() supporting base58/hex-64B/hex-32B seed formats
- Add sign_order_txs_solana() with flexible data unwrapping (kind/data, nested data, flat)
- Add _is_solana_order() auto-detection (chainId=501, chainName=sol, serializedTx)
- Add --private-key-sol CLI argument
- Rename sign_order_txs -> sign_order_txs_evm for clarity
- Main entrypoint auto-routes EVM vs Solana based on chain detection

Signing verified: pubkey matches, sig[1] correctly filled (Ed25519),
sig[0] left empty for relayer. Solana gasless still fails server-side
(relayer does not sign sig[0]), but client signing is correct.

* docs: add Solana signing and order_sign.py usage to wallet-signing.md

- Solana transaction format (shortvec, sig slots, V0 vs Legacy)
- Signer slot mapping (gasless vs user_gas)
- Partial signing pattern with code example
- Key format support (base58/hex-64B/hex-32B)
- Key retrieval from 1Password (sol_key field)
- order_sign.py usage (EVM + Solana), auto-detection, data shapes
- Note: Solana gasless not supported by backend (2026-03-06)

* feat: add wallet setup flow with ephemeral key derivation

- Add wallet_setup.py: generate mnemonic, derive EVM+Solana keys on-the-fly
  - generate: BIP-39 mnemonic (12/24 words)
  - derive: show addresses only (no keys leaked)
  - derive-key: output single chain key to stdout for piping
  - Solana derivation via SLIP-0010 (m/44'/501'/0'/0')
  - EVM derivation via eth_account HD wallet (m/44'/60'/0'/0/0)

- Update SKILL.md: add First-Time Wallet Setup guide
  - Path 1: create new wallet (generate → backup → store mnemonic in 1Password)
  - Path 2: query only (no wallet needed)
  - Security model: mnemonic is only persistent secret, keys derived per-operation

- Update docs/wallet-signing.md: align key management with ephemeral model
  - Mnemonic stored in 1Password, not individual private keys
  - Signing pipeline: 1Password → derive → sign → discard
  - Shell and Python patterns for the derive-sign-cleanup flow

* refactor: wallet setup as domain knowledge, not implementation

- Remove scripts/wallet_setup.py — agents find their own BIP-39/derivation libs
- SKILL.md: First-Time Wallet Setup as principles and rules, not code
  - No 1Password hardcoding — 'secure storage' with requirements
  - Derivation paths table (EVM + Solana)
  - Key management rules (ephemeral keys, never persist, never chat)
- docs/wallet-signing.md: same treatment
  - Secure storage requirements (encrypt at rest, auth to read, no log leak)
  - Conceptual derivation code, not tool-specific
  - Signing pipeline as concept, not shell commands

* fix: mnemonic never displayed in chat, user retrieves via secure storage directly

* docs: update README for Solana signing support and ephemeral key model

- Update dependency description: add solders/base58 for Solana signing
- Update prerequisites: separate query-only vs signing dependencies
- Update Solana Order Mode table: gasless marked 'Not supported'
- Update security sections: ephemeral key model (mnemonic only persistent secret)
- Update future directions: Solana gasless client-ready, pending backend

* refactor: pure-Python Solana signing, zero external dependencies

Remove solders/base58 dependency entirely. Solana signing now uses:
- Pure-Python Ed25519 (RFC 8032): ~100 lines, verified byte-identical
  signatures against solders library
- Pure-Python Base58 (Bitcoin alphabet): encode/decode built-in
- Manual transaction message parsing: shortvec + account_keys extraction

Verified:
- Ed25519 pubkey matches RFC 8032 test vector
- Ed25519 signatures match solders output byte-for-byte
- Real order signed successfully (pubkey 75k14Ug...26NM)
- All 3 keypair formats work (base58, hex-64B, hex-32B)

Top-level imports: argparse, hashlib, json, sys (all stdlib).
eth_account only imported inside EVM functions (lazy).

commit.message:
feat: add x402 payment protocol support (#21)

* feat: add x402 payment protocol support

- docs/x402-payments.md: Domain knowledge covering EIP-3009,
  Permit2, Solana partial-sign, protocol flow, budget controls,
  risk model (credit risk from batch settlement)
- scripts/x402_pay.py: Payment client with sign-eip3009,
  sign-solana, and full HTTP 402 flow (pay subcommand)
- SKILL.md: Extended Domain Knowledge table + Scripts section updated
- CHANGELOG: v2026.3.6-2

* fix: correct EIP-712 signing for x402 facilitator compatibility

- Replace encode_typed_data with manual EIP-712 hash computation
  (encode_typed_data encodes bytes32 differently from facilitators)
- Add _keccak256 and _eip712_hash helpers for correct hashing
- Fix validAfter to use now-600 (clock skew tolerance)
- Fix authorization return values to match signed message exactly
- Add --method and --data params to cmd_pay for POST requests
- Support accepts[] array format in build_payment_payload
- Remove resource field from PaymentPayload (match official SDK)
- Update domain knowledge with signing pitfall, facilitator table,
  PaymentPayload structure, and Pinata testing guide
- Tested: Pinata IPFS upload on Base mainnet - settlement success
  TX: 0x5bbfe577d39da850bd29483b859a7edd07f3a0d92701177d3ed889af7fcca556

* chore: bump version to 2026.3.6-3

* docs: add x402 payments section, design principles, update README

- Add Design Principles table (domain knowledge + tools, zero deps,
  API infrastructure, human-in-the-loop)
- Add x402 Payments section with usage example
- Add x402 Payment row to capabilities table
- Remove testnet validation and other services sections from x402 docs
- Update Security section to reflect zero-external-code principle

* fix: align CHANGELOG version to 2026.3.6-3, move to top

* fix: add --auto safety warning, support X402_PRIVATE_KEY env var

- --auto flag documented as 'testing only, do not use in production agents'
- All subcommands accept X402_PRIVATE_KEY env var as --private-key alternative
- Validation error if neither --private-key nor env var is set

* fix: add $1 hard cap on x402 payment amount

Reject any payment above 1,000,000 units ($1 USDC) in build_payment_payload.
Prevents malicious servers from draining wallet via inflated 402 responses.

commit.message:
refactor: split Domain Knowledge into on-demand modules (#20)

* refactor: split Domain Knowledge into on-demand modules

SKILL.md reduced from 1106 lines (54KB) to 237 lines (12KB), ~78% smaller.
Token cost per session: ~15K → ~3K tokens.

Kept in SKILL.md:
- Skill Domain Knowledge (version awareness)
- Wallet Domain Knowledge (config, amounts, token addresses)
- Extended Domain Knowledge reference table
- Scripts + Safety Rules

Split to docs/:
- docs/wallet-signing.md — key management, BIP-39/44, signing, multi-chain
- docs/market-data.md — security audits, kline, tx info, risky tokens
- docs/trading.md — Order Mode (default), Calldata Mode (legacy), slippage, gas
- docs/other.md — common pitfalls

Agent loads docs on-demand via reference table in SKILL.md.

* refactor: move Common Pitfalls back to SKILL.md, remove docs/other.md

12 lines not worth a separate file — one less on-demand read.

* fix: correct heading hierarchy in docs/ (# → ## → ###)

* refactor: restructure trading.md - split by mode, add common knowledge

- Pre-Trade Workflow: describes both Order Mode and Calldata Mode flows
- Common Trading Knowledge: EVM Approval, Slippage, Gas (shared by both modes)
- Order Mode (Default): all order-* subsections grouped together
- Calldata Mode (Legacy): Swap Quote + Broadcasting moved under it

* docs: note Order Mode gasless auto-handles EVM token approval

* docs: add refund behavior details to Order Status Lifecycle

- Refund chain may differ from source chain
- Refund token may differ from from/to tokens
- Refund amount deducts incurred fees
- txs[] stage field: source/target/refund
- Added refund notification template

commit.message:
docs: add token addresses, cross-chain limits, fix gasless rules (#18)

* docs: add token addresses, cross-chain limits, fix gasless rules

- Add Morph USDT0 contract address
- Add BGB (Bitget Token) addresses for Ethereum and Morph
- Add cross-chain limits table (liqBridge + CCTP per chain)
- Add market field to order confirmation summary
- Fix Solana gasless: not supported (not a bug), features=[] means no no_gas
- Fix gasless rule: only use no_gas when quote returns it in features
- Update cross-chain minimums: Solana $10, Morph $5
- Version bump to 2026.3.5-2

* docs: fix EVM→Sol cross-chain status, Route→Market in templates

- EVM→Sol cross-chain confirmed working (gasless on source chain)
- Sol gasless note updated: only Sol-as-source unsupported
- All confirmation templates: Route → Market
- Calldata mode template also updated

commit.message:
docs: add Order Mode section to README (#17)

- Gasless transactions (EIP-7702): zero gas balance trading
- One-step cross-chain swaps: no manual bridging
- 4 new commands: order-quote/create/submit/status
- Order Mode chain support table
- Usage examples for gasless cross-chain swap

commit.message:
feat: add order mode API (cross-chain + gasless swaps) (#15)

* feat: add order mode API (cross-chain + gasless swaps)

- Add 4 new commands: order-quote, order-create, order-submit, order-status
- Support cross-chain swaps (fromChain != toChain)
- Support gasless mode (no_gas feature)
- Support EIP-7702 signature mode
- Support B2B fee splitting (feeRate)
- Add Morph chain support
- Update SKILL.md with full order mode domain knowledge
- Bump version to 2026.3.4-1

* docs: default to gasless mode, clarify toAmount estimation

- Default to no_gas when features includes 'no_gas' (don't ask user)
- Clarify toAmount is pre-gas estimate, receiveAmount is actual
- Update confirmation summary templates

* docs: order-create before present, auto-apply gasless

- Reorder workflow: create order first, then present as 'contract'
- User sees actual order (orderId, tx count) before confirming
- Gasless mode auto-applied when available, no user choice needed
- Updated confirmation summary templates with orderId

* docs: allow user to override gasless with 'use my gas'

- Default remains gasless when available
- User can opt out by saying 'use my gas' / 'user gas' etc.
- Added third gas mode display rule for user override

* feat: add order signing helper + signing domain knowledge

- Add scripts/order_sign.py: handles both signatures (gasless) and txs modes
- Critical insight: use API-provided hash field, do NOT recompute EIP-712
- Document backend relayer flow (agent signs, relayer constructs + broadcasts)
- Document signature format (65 bytes, v=27/28, not y_parity)

* docs: update domain knowledge from real-world testing

- Gasless: document EIP-7702 relayer flow, features field unreliability
- Signatures mode: detailed response structure with both sig types explained
- Known issues: cross-chain min amount, Base 80000, EIP-712 hash mismatch, order expiry
- Security: trust model table, pre-sign verification checklist, MITM note
- Auto-detection: try no_gas when wallet has zero native balance regardless of features

* docs: add Wallet & Signing domain knowledge section

- Key hierarchy: BIP-39/44, EVM shared key, Solana separate
- Key management: minimal privilege, 1Password only, no mnemonic for agents
- Signature types: raw tx, EIP-191, EIP-712, EIP-7702 with when-to-use guide
- unsafe_sign_hash vs sign_message distinction (critical for API hashes)
- Multi-chain signing: secp256k1 vs Ed25519
- Transaction anatomy: type 0/2/4 field breakdown

* docs: enforce confirmation flow, add small amount warning, 7702 binding state

- MANDATORY: agent must present confirmation and wait for user approval
- Skipping confirmation is a violation of operating rules
- Small amount gasless warning: <$1 trades lose ~15% to gas
- Gas overhead table by input amount
- EIP-7702 binding state table: 2 sigs first time, 1 sig after
- Enhanced confirmation template with EIP-712 verification section
- Pre-trade workflow explicitly marks PRESENT and WAIT as required steps

* docs: gasless toAmount warning - quote estimate not actual

- Confirmation summary: add gasless gas deduction warning line
- Clarify toAmount is from quote, not create (create has no toAmount)
- Fees line now says '不含 gas' for gasless mode
- Explain the data flow: quote→create→status and where amounts come from

* docs: overhaul order mode workflow with complete lifecycle

Workflow improvements:
- Add step 4: order-status after create to get accurate toAmount
- Use order-status.toAmount (not quote) for confirmation summary
- Add step 8-10: notify user, poll result, show completion with txId
- Block explorer URLs for all 8 supported chains
- Poll timing: same-chain 10s/2min max, cross-chain 15s/5min max

toAmount accuracy:
- Document three sources of truth: quote, status(init), status(success)
- order-status toAmount is more accurate than quote — always use it

Confirmation templates:
- Same-chain gasless example with real data
- Cross-chain gasless example (Base→Polygon)
- Normal gas mode example
- Note: toAmount now from order-status, not quote

Completion messages:
- Success format with receiveAmount + txId + explorer link
- Failed format with troubleshooting hints

order-status response:
- Document all fields and when they're available
- txs array contains chain, txId, stage on success

* docs: add duplicate execution pitfall, cross-chain completion template

- Pitfall #8: never duplicate order execution (irreversible, double spend)
- Pitfall #9: cross-chain orders return 2 TXs (source + target)
- Completion template: separate same-chain and cross-chain formats
- Cross-chain shows both explorer links (source + target chain)

* docs: gasless-first strategy, chain support table, fallback detection

- Always try gasless first (--feature no_gas on every trade)
- Detect via response: signatures=gasless, txs=normal fallback
- If fallback + no native token → stop and warn user
- Gasless chain support table: Base ✅, BNB ❌, Polygon ❌
- Don't rely on features field or eip7702 flags — just try it
- Updated Gas Mode section with detection logic

* docs: update gasless chain table - all EVM chains supported

- All EVM chains marked as gasless supported
- Cross-chain requires source chain 7702 binding first
- Same-chain swap binds 7702, then cross-chain gasless works

* docs: single poll after submit, never block agent; Solana gasless supported

- Poll once (10s) after submit, return immediately if still processing
- Never loop/block waiting for completion — cross-chain can take 5-15min
- User can check order status later manually
- Solana marked as gasless supported in chain table
- Workflow reduced from 10 steps to 8 steps

* docs: critical pitfall - cross-chain toAddress must match target chain format

- EVM→EVM: same address works
- EVM→Solana: must use Solana Base58 address
- EVM→Tron: must use Tron T... address
- Wrong format causes stuck funds or refund, API won't reject upfront

* docs: toAddress required in quote for non-EVM cross-chain targets

- order-quote also needs toAddress for EVM→Sol/Tron, not just create
- Missing toAddress causes 80000, not a missing route

* docs: mark Solana gasless as not working, pending API fix

- Order mode gasless on Solana: submit returns status=0 but execution
  always fails (tested 6+ orders)
- Signing verified correct (nacl VerifyKey passes)
- Cross-chain to-sol also has known bug (API team aware)
- Solana same-chain calldata mode works but needs SOL for gas

* chore: bump version to 2026.3.5-1, add changelog for Solana findings

* chore: merge 3.4 and 3.5 changelog into single 2026.3.5-1 release

* chore: fix SKILL.md frontmatter version to 2026.3.5-1

* feat: add version management (YYYY.M.DD) and CHANGELOG.md

- Add version/updated fields to SKILL.md frontmatter (2026.3.2)
- Add Version Awareness section to Domain Knowledge
- Create CHANGELOG.md with full history and security audit per version
- Agent notifies user on first load or version change

* docs: clarify multi-release versioning format (YYYY.M.DD-N)

* docs: always include suffix in version (YYYY.M.DD-N)

* feat: daily version check, upgrade prompt, and post-upgrade security audit

- Agent checks for new version on first daily use
- Prompts user with version diff and changelog summary
- Guided upgrade via curl from GitHub raw
- Post-upgrade: diffs scripts, flags suspicious changes

* docs: reduce version check frequency to weekly (max once per 7 days)

* fix: replace Chinese text with English in upgrade prompt template

* fix: simplify upgrade to git pull instead of individual curl downloads

* fix: describe upgrade intent, not specific commands — let agent decide how

* feat: add platform-level security audit step to post-upgrade check

---------

commit.message:
feat: first-time swap configuration & strengthened security guidance (#13)

* feat: add first-time swap configuration and strengthen security guidance

- Add First-Time Swap Configuration section: deadline preference + security check toggle
- Default deadline changed from 600s to 300s for better sandwich attack protection
- Security checks now mandatory for unfamiliar tokens regardless of user preference
- Addresses SlowMist CISO feedback on swap deadline and optional security audit
- Ref: https://cryptonews.net/news/security/32491385/

* feat: add --deadline parameter to swap-calldata command

Verified: API accepts deadline in request body and encodes it into calldata.
Response 'deadline' field always shows 600 (display only), but actual
on-chain deadline uses the provided value. Tested with 120s on BSC.

---------

Co-authored-by: bitget-dev-pub-agent <bitget-dev-pub-agent@bitget.com>

commit.message:
docs: add domain knowledge section to SKILL.md (#6)

* docs: add domain knowledge section to SKILL.md

Add structured domain knowledge that goes beyond command syntax:
- Swap flow sequence constraints (quote → calldata → sign → send)
- Swap quote response interpretation (estimateRevert, market field)
- Security audit field meanings and recommended actions
- K-line valid periods and tx-info valid intervals
- Historical coins pagination pattern
- Native token address convention
- Common pitfalls (chain codes, batch format, LP lock)
- Complete command reference (added batch-tx-info, history, swap-send)

This separates concerns: CLI README documents what commands exist,
SKILL.md documents how to use them correctly together.

* docs: add trading workflow, risk assessment, slippage and gas guidance

Expand Domain Knowledge with practical trading experience:
- Market data combination patterns (token-info + tx-info + liquidity)
- Pre-trade checklist: 8-step flow from security audit to broadcast
- Risky token identification: multi-signal red flag table
- Slippage estimation method and threshold guidelines
- Gas/fee awareness per chain with cost considerations
- Additional pitfalls: stale quotes, insufficient gas

* docs: add auto-slippage guidance and --slippage parameter

- Document BGW's built-in auto-slippage in quote response
- Add slippage threshold guidelines (≤3% ok, 3-10% warn, >10% danger)
- Document --slippage override and toMinAmount alternative
- Separate slippage tolerance from price impact estimation
- Add --slippage example to swap-calldata command reference

* docs: add EVM approval, deadline, streamlined UX, slippage clarity

Lessons from real swap test (0.1 USDC → USDT on BNB Chain):

- Add EVM Token Approval section: missing approval is #1 cause of
  failed swaps, wastes gas on reverted tx
- Add transaction deadline parameter docs (default 600s, customizable)
- Redesign Pre-Trade Workflow: agent runs checks silently, presents
  single confirmation summary instead of prompting at every step
- Well-known tokens get streamlined flow; risky tokens get verbose warnings
- Clarify slippage tolerance vs price impact (different concepts)
- Add slippage threshold table for quick reference
- New pitfalls: missing approval, automate boring parts

* docs: add swap-send complete CLI workflow to domain knowledge

---------

Co-authored-by: bitget-dev-pub-agent <bitget-dev-pub-agent@bitget.com>

commit.message:
docs: clarify swap amount is human-readable, not raw units (#5)

BREAKING MISCONCEPTION: Swap API fromAmount uses human-readable values
(e.g. '0.1' for 0.1 USDT), NOT smallest units (wei/lamports).

This differs from most on-chain APIs and caused failed transactions
when agents passed raw unit values like '100000000000000000'.

- Add prominent warning in SKILL.md, README, CLI help, and MCP docstrings
- Prevents other AI agents from making the same mistake

Co-authored-by: bitget-dev-pub-agent <bitget-dev-pub-agent@bitget.com>

committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772097113 +0800

commit.message:
docs: unify README format, add security section, consistency fixes (#2)

- Standardize Supported Chains format across all repos
- Add Security section to CLI README

commit.message: Initial commit

readme_html:
Bitget Wallet Skill

Overview

An AI Agent skill that wraps the Bitget Wallet API, enabling natural-language-driven on-chain data queries and swap operations.

Design Principles

Principle Description
Domain Knowledge + Tools Not just API wrappers — includes trading workflows, signing guides, security models, and known pitfalls so agents make informed decisions
Zero External Dependencies All code is self-contained. Solana signing is pure Python (Ed25519 + base58 built-in). EVM signing uses eth_account (standard). Only requests for API calls. No pip install needed for Solana
API Infrastructure, Not Reimplementation Capabilities come from Bitget Wallet's production API. The skill provides the knowledge and tooling layer, not a parallel implementation
Human-in-the-Loop by Default Swap operations generate transaction data but never sign autonomously. User confirmation required for all fund-moving actions

Core Capabilities

Capability Description Example
Token Info Price, market cap, holders, social links (twitter/website/telegram) "What's the price of SOL?"
Batch Price Query Multi-token price lookup in one call Portfolio valuation
K-line Data 1s/1m/5m/15m/30m/1h/4h/1d/1w candlestick data (with buy/sell breakdown) Trend analysis, charting
Transaction Stats 5m/1h/4h/24h buy/sell volume & trader count Activity detection, whale monitoring
Rankings Top gainers / top losers / hot picks Market scanning, alpha discovery
Liquidity Pools LP pool information Slippage estimation, depth analysis
Security Audit Contract safety checks (honeypot, tax, permissions, blacklist, dev rug history, bundle tx) Pre-trade risk control
Batch Tx Info Batch transaction statistics for multiple tokens "Compare volume for SOL and ETH"
Historical Coins Discover new tokens by timestamp "What tokens launched today?"
Swap Send Broadcast signed transactions with MEV protection "Broadcast my signed swap"
Swap Quote Best-route quote for cross-chain/same-chain swaps "How much USDC for 1 SOL?"
Swap Calldata Generate unsigned transaction data Execute trades via wallet signing
Order Quote Cross-chain + gasless aware price quote "Quote 10 USDC Base to BNB USDT"
Order Create Create order with unsigned tx/signature data One-step cross-chain swap
Order Submit Submit signed transactions for an order Gasless or normal execution
Order Status Track order lifecycle (init→processing→success) "Check my swap status"
x402 Payment Pay for x402-enabled APIs with USDC on Base "Access this paid API endpoint"

⚠️ Swap amounts are human-readable — pass 0.1 for 0.1 USDT, NOT 100000000000000000. The toAmount in responses is also human-readable. This differs from most on-chain APIs.

✨ Order Mode — Gasless & Cross-Chain Swaps

Order Mode is the key upgrade in v2026.3.5. It enables two capabilities no other AI agent swap skill offers:

⛽ Gasless Transactions (EIP-7702)

  • Swap tokens with zero native token balance — no ETH, no BNB, no MATIC needed
  • Gas cost is deducted from the input token automatically
  • Agent only signs; a backend relayer pays gas and broadcasts the transaction
  • Supported on all 7 chains: Ethereum, Solana, BNB Chain, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Morph (min $5 USD, Morph $1)

🌉 One-Step Cross-Chain Swaps

  • Swap tokens across different chains in a single order — no manual bridging
  • Example: USDC on Base → USDT on BNB Chain, one API call, one signature
  • Combined with gasless: cross-chain swap with zero gas on the source chain

How it works:

1. order-quote   → Get price + check gasless support
2. order-create  → Create order, receive unsigned data
3. Sign          → Agent signs with wallet key (EIP-712 for gasless, raw tx for normal)
4. order-submit  → Submit signed data
5. order-status  → Track until success

Example — Gasless cross-chain swap:

# Quote: Base USDC → BNB USDT
python3 scripts/bitget_api.py order-quote \
  --from-chain base --from-contract 0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913 \
  --to-chain bnb --to-contract 0x55d398326f99059fF775485246999027B3197955 \
  --amount 10 --from-address 0xYourAddress --to-address 0xYourAddress

# Create order with gasless
python3 scripts/bitget_api.py order-create \
  --from-chain base --from-contract 0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913 \
  --to-chain bnb --to-contract 0x55d398326f99059fF775485246999027B3197955 \
  --amount 10 --from-address 0xYourAddress --to-address 0xYourAddress \
  --market bkbridgev3.liqbridge --slippage 1 --feature no_gas

💳 x402 Payments — Pay-Per-Request API Access

x402 is an open standard for HTTP-native payments. When an agent encounters a paid API (HTTP 402), it signs a USDC authorization and retries — no accounts, no API keys needed.

How it works:

1. Agent requests a resource → gets HTTP 402 + payment requirements
2. Agent signs EIP-3009 TransferWithAuthorization (gasless, off-chain)
3. Agent retries with PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header
4. Service's facilitator settles on-chain → agent gets the resource

Key features:

  • Truly gasless — agent pays only USDC, facilitator sponsors gas
  • No accounts needed — wallet address is your identity
  • Works with any x402 service — Pinata IPFS, DiamondClaws DeFi data, and 100+ more
# Example: pay $0.001 for Pinata IPFS upload
python3 scripts/x402_pay.py pay \
  --url "https://402.pinata.cloud/v1/pin/private?fileSize=100" \
  --private-key <key> --method POST --data '{"fileSize": 100}' --auto

See docs/x402-payments.md for domain knowledge, signing details, and testing guide.

Supported Chains

Order Mode (swap + gasless): Ethereum · Solana · BNB Chain · Base · Arbitrum · Polygon · Morph (all support gasless with $5 min, Morph $1)

Market Data (32 chains): Ethereum · Solana · BNB Chain · Base · Arbitrum · Polygon · Tron · TON · Sui · Optimism · Morph · Fantom · AVAX · Berachain · Sonic · Linea · Blast · Aptos · Hyperliquid · and more

Architecture

Natural Language Input
    ↓
AI Agent (OpenClaw / Dify / Custom)
    ↓
bitget_api.py (Python 3.11+)
    ↓  ← Built-in demo keys or env var override
HMAC-SHA256 Signing
    ↓
Bitget Wallet API (bopenapi.bgwapi.io)
    ↓
Structured JSON → Agent interprets → Natural language response

Security by Design:

  • Built-in demo credentials are public read-only API keys (safe to share)
  • For production use, override with your own keys via BGW_API_KEY / BGW_API_SECRET env vars
  • Swap calldata generates transaction data; signing requires explicit wallet key access
  • Wallet key management: mnemonic stored in secure storage, private keys derived on-the-fly and discarded after each signing operation (never persisted)

Agent Use Cases

1. Personal Research Assistant

"Check if this Solana meme coin is safe, and give me a price quote."

  • Token info + security audit + price in a single query
  • For: individual traders, researchers
  • Platforms: Telegram Bot, Discord Bot, OpenClaw

2. Portfolio Management Agent

"What's my total portfolio value right now?"

  • Batch query across chains and tokens, calculate net value
  • Scheduled snapshots + K-line data for historical tracking
  • For: DeFi users, fund managers
  • Platforms: OpenClaw cron + Telegram alerts

3. Market Monitoring / Alert Agent

Automatically scan top gainers, detect anomalies, push alerts

  • Rankings (topGainers / topLosers / Hotpicks) + transaction volume + security audit combined
  • Use Hotpicks for curated trending tokens, topGainers for momentum plays
  • Discover trending tokens → auto-run security audit → filter honeypots → notify user
  • For: on-chain alpha hunters
  • Platforms: Cron jobs, Dify workflows

4. Semi-Automated Trading Agent

"Buy this token with 1 SOL"

  • Swap quote → show route and slippage → user confirms → generate calldata → wallet signs
  • Human-in-the-loop — the agent cannot sign independently
  • For: active traders wanting an AI assistant
  • Platforms: OpenClaw + Bitget Wallet App / hardware wallet

5. Arbitrage Bot Data Layer

Monitor DEX price discrepancies, discover cross-chain arbitrage opportunities

  • Multi-chain swap-quote comparison, calculate spreads
  • Combine with CEX data for DEX-CEX spread monitoring
  • For: quant teams
  • Platforms: Custom Python scripts, OpenClaw sub-agents

6. Community Service Bot

Someone asks "How much is XX coin?" in a group chat — bot auto-replies

  • Lightweight queries, fast response
  • Security audit feature doubles as anti-scam protection
  • For: Telegram/Discord communities
  • Platforms: Telegram Bot + OpenClaw skill

7. Dify / LangChain Tool Node

Integrate as a Tool in Dify workflows or LangChain agents

  • bitget_api.py can serve directly as a Dify Code node or external API Tool
  • Can also be wrapped as an MCP Server for any MCP-compatible agent framework
  • For: enterprise agent platform integration

Quick Start

Prerequisites

  1. Python 3.11+
  2. requests library (pip install requests)
  3. For EVM signing: eth-account (pip install eth-account)
  4. Public demo API credentials are built in. To use your own keys, set BGW_API_KEY and BGW_API_SECRET env vars.

Solana signing requires no additional packages — pure Python Ed25519 and base58 are built into order_sign.py.

Note: The built-in demo keys are for testing purposes and may change over time. If they stop working, please update the skill (git pull) to get the latest keys.

Examples

# Get SOL price
python3 scripts/bitget_api.py token-price --chain sol --contract ""

# Security audit for a token
python3 scripts/bitget_api.py security --chain sol --contract <contract_address>

# Swap quote (1 SOL → USDC)
python3 scripts/bitget_api.py swap-quote \
  --from-chain sol --from-contract "" \
  --to-contract EPjFWdd5AufqSSqeM2qN1xzybapC8G4wEGGkZwyTDt1v \
  --amount 1

Supported Chains (Order Mode)

Chain Same-chain Cross-chain Gasless
Ethereum ✅ ✅ ✅
BNB Chain ✅ ✅ ✅
Base ✅ ✅ ✅
Arbitrum ✅ ✅ ✅
Polygon ✅ ✅ ✅
Morph ✅ ✅ ✅
Solana ✅ ✅ ✅ (min ~$5-6 USD)

Calldata mode (non-order) supports additional chains: Tron, TON, Sui, Optimism, and more.

Future Directions

Direction Description
Solana Gasless Pending backend support — client signing is ready, awaiting relayer implementation
On-chain Event Subscription WebSocket listeners for large transactions, new pool creation
Historical Data Cache Store K-line + price data in local SQLite to reduce API calls
Multi-wallet Management Support multi-address balance queries and batch quotes
Risk Rule Engine Security audit results + custom rules (blacklist, min liquidity thresholds)

Compatible Platforms

✅ Tested & Verified

Platform Status Notes
OpenClaw ✅ Passed Native skill support
Manus ✅ Passed Auto-installed and executed
Bolt.new ✅ Passed Auto-cloned repo, ran all commands
Devin ✅ Passed Read SKILL.md, installed deps, returned correct data
Replit Agent ✅ Passed Full project setup with web frontend

🔧 Should Work (file system + Python + network access)

Platform Type How to Use
Claude Code CLI Agent Clone repo, add SKILL.md to project context
Codex CLI CLI Agent Clone repo, reference in AGENTS.md
Cursor IDE Agent Clone into project, or use MCP version
Windsurf IDE Agent Clone into project, or use MCP version
Cline VS Code Agent Clone into project workspace
Aider CLI Agent Add scripts to project
OpenHands Coding Agent Docker sandbox with full file system
SWE-agent Coding Agent Shell access in sandbox
Dify Workflow Platform Use as Code node or external API Tool
Coze Agent Platform Import as plugin or API Tool
LangChain / CrewAI Frameworks Wrap bitget_api.py as a Tool

💡 Compatibility Rule

Any AI agent that can read files + run Python + access the internet should work with this skill.

Related Projects

  • bitget-wallet-mcp — MCP Server for Claude Desktop / Cursor / Windsurf
  • bitget-wallet-cli — CLI tool for terminal users

Security Notes

  • Built-in demo API keys are public and read-only; for production, use env vars (BGW_API_KEY / BGW_API_SECRET)
  • Swap functions generate quotes and transaction data — signing requires explicit wallet access
  • Wallet mnemonic is the only persistent secret; private keys are derived per-operation and discarded
  • Large operations require explicit user confirmation (human-in-the-loop)
  • Always run a security audit (security command) before interacting with any token

Security

  • Zero external dependencies for Solana — pure Python Ed25519 (RFC 8032) and base58 built into order_sign.py. EVM uses eth_account. No obscure packages, no supply-chain risk.
  • Only communicates with https://bopenapi.bgwapi.io (BGW API) and x402 resource servers — no other external endpoints
  • No eval() / exec() or dynamic code execution
  • No file system access outside the skill directory
  • Built-in API keys are public demo credentials (safe to commit)
  • No data collection, telemetry, or analytics
  • No access to sensitive files (SSH keys, credentials, wallet files, etc.)
  • We recommend auditing the source yourself before installation
